summary: crypto-promiser typosquats crypto-promise and drops a remote-code payload at install and require time
details: crypto-promiser@1.0.2 is a one-character typosquat of the legitimate crypto-promise package and reproduces its README/API surface (the README badge even links back to crypto-promise on npm). Beyond mirroring the target, the package ships a dropper: prepinstall.js holds a base64 constant deceptively named HASH_KEY that decodes to https://api.jsonbin.io/v3/b/689dcd6043b1c97be91e2636, fetches JSON via axios, and pipes the.data.record.cache field into a detached `node` child process via stdin (spawn('node', [], { detached: true,... }); child.stdin.write(...); child.unref()). This runs on npm install via the postinstall lifecycle script, and index.js also invokes it from a top-level IIFE that dynamically imports./prepinstall.js, so the same remote fetch-and-execute fires whenever a consumer require()s the package. The jsonbin.io bin is attacker-controlled and mutable, so the executed payload can change at any time without a new npm publish.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
